18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime is the SD-024 successor to ExecuteBeadLoopOptions.
19	// Durable knobs (Assignee, ReviewMaxRetries, NoProgressCooldown,
20	// MaxNoChangesBeforeClose, HeartbeatInterval, Harness, Model, Provider,
21	// ModelRef, Profile, MinTier, MaxTier) are stripped — they live on
22	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via RunWithConfig's rcfg argument.
23	// Only non-serializable plumbing and per-invocation runtime intent remain.
24	//
25	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Bead 6.
26	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
27		Log          io.Writer
28		EventSink    io.Writer
29		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
30		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
31		Once         bool
32		PollInterval time.Duration
33		NoReview     bool
34		LabelFilter  string
35		SessionID    string
36		WorkerID     string
37		ProjectRoot  string
38	}

40	// RunWithConfig is the SD-024 successor to Run. It accepts a sealed
41	// ResolvedConfig (durable knobs) and a ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime (plumbing
42	// + per-invocation intent) and delegates to Run under the hood. Behavior
43	// is identical to Run with an equivalently-populated ExecuteBeadLoopOptions.
44	//
45	// Stage 1 of SD-024: this method exists alongside Run; production callers
46	// have not migrated yet.
47	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) RunWithConfig(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
48		opts := ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{
49			Assignee:                rcfg.Assignee(),
50			Once:                    runtime.Once,
51			PollInterval:            runtime.PollInterval,
52			NoProgressCooldown:      rcfg.NoProgressCooldown(),
53			MaxNoChangesBeforeClose: rcfg.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose(),
54			HeartbeatInterval:       rcfg.HeartbeatInterval(),
55			Log:                     runtime.Log,
56			NoReview:                runtime.NoReview,
57			ReviewMaxRetries:        rcfg.ReviewMaxRetries(),
58			EventSink:               runtime.EventSink,
59			ProgressCh:              runtime.ProgressCh,
60			PreClaimHook:            runtime.PreClaimHook,
61			WorkerID:                runtime.WorkerID,
62			ProjectRoot:             runtime.ProjectRoot,
63			Harness:                 rcfg.Harness(),
64			Model:                   rcfg.Model(),
65			Profile:                 rcfg.Profile(),
66			Provider:                rcfg.Provider(),
67			ModelRef:                rcfg.ModelRef(),
68			SessionID:               runtime.SessionID,
69			LabelFilter:             runtime.LabelFilter,
70			MinTier:                 rcfg.MinTier(),
71			MaxTier:                 rcfg.MaxTier(),
72		}
73		return w.Run(ctx, opts)
74	}

211		Once                    bool
212		PollInterval            time.Duration
213		NoProgressCooldown      time.Duration
214		MaxNoChangesBeforeClose int
215		// HeartbeatInterval, if > 0, overrides bead.HeartbeatInterval for this
216		// worker's claim heartbeat loop. Tests use this to shorten the tick.

221		// tight iteration loops where review latency is not acceptable.
222		NoReview bool

224		// ReviewMaxRetries caps the number of reviewer attempts per committed
225		// result_rev before the loop emits a terminal `review-manual-required`
226		// event and parks the bead (FEAT-022 §14). Zero or negative falls back
227		// to DefaultReviewMaxRetries (3). The counter is scoped to a single
228		// result_rev: a fresh result_rev resets the counter, and iterations
229		// without a committed result_rev (--no-merge, execution-failed) do not
230		// consume the budget because the reviewer is not invoked.
231		ReviewMaxRetries int
232	
233		// EventSink receives structured JSONL progress events emitted at
234		// loop.start, bead.claimed, bead.result, and loop.end milestones.
235		// When nil, no structured events are written. Log (terminal text)
236		// is independent and still emitted for human operators.
237		EventSink io.Writer

240		// bead execution managed by this loop. The caller is responsible for

243		// caller (WorkerManager.runWorker) closes it after Run returns.

23			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {

28					SessionID: "sess-1",
29					ResultRev: "deadbeef",
30				}, nil
31			}),
32		}

34		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
35		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
36		result, err := worker.RunWithConfig(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{Once: true})

5	// TestLoopConfigOpts names every durable knob the execute-bead loop
6	// reads via ResolvedConfig. Tests must specify each field explicitly;
7	// there are no zero-value defaults that silently bypass real config.
8	//
9	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Test config constructors.
10	type TestLoopConfigOpts struct {
11		Assignee                string
12		ReviewMaxRetries        int
13		NoProgressCooldown      time.Duration
14		MaxNoChangesBeforeClose int
15		HeartbeatInterval       time.Duration
16		Harness                 string
17		Model                   string
18		Profile                 string
19		MinTier                 string
20		MaxTier                 string
21		EvidenceCaps            EvidenceCapsConfig
22	}
23	
24	// NewTestConfigForLoop returns a *Config that, when Resolve()d with the
25	// matching CLIOverrides, produces a ResolvedConfig whose loop-relevant
26	// accessors return exactly the values supplied in opts.
27	//
28	// Tests must obtain ResolvedConfig via Resolve on the returned *Config
29	// — there is no shortcut to construct a ResolvedConfig directly. Pure
30	// CLI-override fields (Assignee, Profile, MinTier, MaxTier) are passed
31	// through CLIOverrides at the call site; the constructor stores every
32	// other field on *Config so it round-trips through Resolve.
33	func NewTestConfigForLoop(opts TestLoopConfigOpts) *Config {
34		reviewMaxRetries := opts.ReviewMaxRetries
35		maxNoChanges := opts.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose
36	
37		caps := opts.EvidenceCaps
38	
39		return &Config{
40			Version: "1.0",
41			Agent: &AgentConfig{
42				Harness: opts.Harness,
43				Model:   opts.Model,
44			},
45			Workers: &WorkersConfig{
46				NoProgressCooldown:      opts.NoProgressCooldown.String(),
47				MaxNoChangesBeforeClose: &maxNoChanges,
48				HeartbeatInterval:       opts.HeartbeatInterval.String(),
49			},
50			ReviewMaxRetries: &reviewMaxRetries,
51			EvidenceCaps:     &caps,
52		}
53	}
54	
55	// TestLoopOverrides returns the CLIOverrides that, combined with the
56	// *Config produced by NewTestConfigForLoop(opts), drive a Resolve call
57	// to a ResolvedConfig matching opts. Pure-override fields (Assignee,
58	// Profile, MinTier, MaxTier) have no durable home on *Config; they are
59	// applied at Resolve time only.
60	func TestLoopOverrides(opts TestLoopConfigOpts) CLIOverrides {
61		return CLIOverrides{
62			Assignee: opts.Assignee,
63			Profile:  opts.Profile,
64			MinTier:  opts.MinTier,
65			MaxTier:  opts.MaxTier,
66		}
67	}

678				callCount++

680				// and no cooldown is applied — bead stays immediately retryable.

701		got, err := store.Get(b.ID)
702		require.NoError(t, err)

816	// TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesDoesNotStarveQueue verifies that a bead with
817	// repeated no_changes results cannot prevent other ready beads from being
818	// executed across multiple queue passes. It also verifies the full adjudication
819	// lifecycle: other beads run unblocked while the no-changes bead is open, and
820	// the no-changes bead is eventually closed as already_satisfied at the threshold.
821	func TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesDoesNotStarveQueue(t *testing.T) {
822		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
823		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
824	
825		// Three beads: one that always returns no_changes (no cooldown), two that succeed.
826		// Not supplying BaseRev/ResultRev means shouldSuppressNoProgress returns false,
827		// so no retry-after is written and the bead stays immediately retryable between
828		// passes. This keeps the test deterministic without mocking time.
829		ncBead := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-nc10", Title: "Always no-changes", Priority: 0}
830		work1 := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-wk11", Title: "Work bead 1", Priority: 1}
831		work2 := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-wk12", Title: "Work bead 2", Priority: 2}
832		require.NoError(t, store.Create(ncBead))
833		require.NoError(t, store.Create(work1))
834		require.NoError(t, store.Create(work2))
835	
836		var mu sync.Mutex
837		executed := []string{}
838	
839		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
840			Store: store,
841			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
842				mu.Lock()
843				executed = append(executed, beadID)
844				mu.Unlock()
845				if beadID == ncBead.ID {
846					// No BaseRev/ResultRev → shouldSuppressNoProgress returns false
847					// → no cooldown written → bead is immediately retryable.
848					return ExecuteBeadReport{
849						BeadID: beadID,
850						Status: ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges,
851					}, nil
852				}
853				return ExecuteBeadReport{
854					BeadID:    beadID,
855					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
856					SessionID: "sess-" + beadID,
857					ResultRev: "bbbb",
858				}, nil
859			}),
860		}
861	
862		const threshold = 2
863		opts := ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{
864			Assignee:                "worker",
865			MaxNoChangesBeforeClose: threshold,
866		}
867	
868		// Pass 1: all three beads are ready.
869		// ncBead returns no_changes (count=1 < threshold) and stays open.
870		// work1 and work2 succeed and are closed.
871		// The `attempted` map inside Run prevents ncBead from being picked up
872		// a second time within the same pass — the other beads run unblocked.
873		result1, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), opts)
874		require.NoError(t, err)
875		assert.Equal(t, 3, result1.Attempts, "all three beads must be attempted in pass 1")
876		assert.Equal(t, 2, result1.Successes)
877		assert.Equal(t, 1, result1.Failures)
878		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges, result1.LastFailureStatus)
879	
880		w1, _ := store.Get(work1.ID)
881		w2, _ := store.Get(work2.ID)
882		nc, _ := store.Get(ncBead.ID)
883		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, w1.Status)
884		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, w2.Status)
885		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, nc.Status, "ncBead must stay open after first no_changes")
886	
887		// Pass 2: only ncBead remains; count reaches threshold → closed as already_satisfied.
888		result2, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), opts)
889		require.NoError(t, err)
890		assert.Equal(t, 1, result2.Attempts)
891		assert.Equal(t, 1, result2.Successes, "ncBead must be closed as already_satisfied")
892		require.Len(t, result2.Results, 1)
893		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied, result2.Results[0].Status)
894	
895		nc, _ = store.Get(ncBead.ID)
896		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, nc.Status)
897	
898		// Pass 3: queue is empty.
899		result3, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), opts)
900		require.NoError(t, err)
901		assert.True(t, result3.NoReadyWork)
902		assert.Equal(t, 0, result3.Attempts)
903	
904		// ncBead was attempted exactly twice (once per pass), never a third time.
905		ncExec := 0
906		for _, id := range executed {
907			if id == ncBead.ID {
908				ncExec++
909			}
910		}
911		assert.Equal(t, 2, ncExec, "ncBead must be executed exactly twice across all passes")
912	}
